payload.description:
Observed behavior: model frontier can complete with no_data rows when the feedback-derived record set is empty.

Expected behavior: because this procedure compares cost vs accuracy, a run with no labeled records should fail loudly before dispatching evaluations. Silent no_data completion is misleading in prototype mode.

Feature: Model frontier requires labeled comparison records

Scenario: Score has no labeled records
Given a model frontier run for a score with no eligible feedback-derived labeled records
When the procedure prepares evaluations
Then the procedure fails with a clear message that no labeled records are available
And it does not dispatch candidate evaluations.

Scenario: Score has labeled records
Given a model frontier run with eligible labeled records
When the procedure prepares evaluations
Then it dispatches evaluations for the planned variants.
payload.title: Fail model frontier loudly when no labeled records exist
